why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:clear_dialog must dispatch through Stevenarella packet_by_id using the official framed packet id and official empty body generated by the oracle answer. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for the official singleton empty-body fixture only, not dialog UI clearing behavior, screen flow, Configuration completion, Play entry, world load, render readiness, or runtime behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_clear_dialog_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 17, official empty body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x11.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ClientboundClearDialogPacket.INSTANCE, ClientboundClearDialogPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundClearDialogPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundClearDialogPacket.type().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound clear_dialog dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound mapping contains cookie_request id 0x00 through server_links id 0x10, but not clear_dialog id 0x11.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0x11 in Clientbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration clientbound clear_dialog packet id 17
